_instructions: This file draws the 'Lower Logan River' schematic page (Dewitt Springs down to Cutler Terminus).
See public/schematics/README.md for the full field reference and a longer walkthrough - this is the quick version.
This page intentionally RECAPS the Dewitt Springs Junction, the Dewitt Springs tributary, and the Water Lab station at its
own top (same node ids as in upper-logan.json) so the page reads as a complete, standalone diagram even though those same
live stations already appear at the bottom of the Upper Logan Canyon page. This duplication is deliberate, not a bug.
NODES: each node is a card on the diagram, listed in the order the water actually flows.
id         - unique within this file. Tributaries/diversions/links reference another node's id via connectsTo.
name       - for kind 'mainstem'/'tributary'/'diversion', must fuzzy-match a live station's display name from
HydroServer/DWRi/USGS, unless stationId is set, in which case that always wins. For 'junction'/'link'
nodes it is just label text.
stationId  - optional but recommended: the exact station id/code to match instead of guessing from name - the same id
used in config/station_config.yaml (usgs_stations/dwri_stations), or a HydroServer station code (a
display_names key, e.g. 'BC_CONF_A'). See README.md for details.
kind       - 'mainstem' (a real gauge station on the main channel), 'junction' (an invisible confluence point on the main
channel, no card - where a tributary/diversion attaches), 'tributary' (water flowing IN), 'diversion' (water
flowing OUT), or 'link' (a card with no live data of its own that just navigates to another schematic page).
connectsTo - tributary/diversion/link only: the id of the mainstem/junction node this attaches to, or another
tributary/diversion/link's id to chain onto. A tributary/diversion can connect straight to a mainstem
node (not just a junction) if that's where it actually attaches - see dwri_nwfield -> mainstreet, or
little_bear/spring_creek_mendon -> terminus, below.
side       - tributary/diversion/link only, when connectsTo is set: 'left' or 'right', which side of the main channel
to draw the card on.
colorGroup - optional; must match a name from waterway_groups or schematic_groups in config/station_config.yaml.
linkTo     - optional on any node; the schematic page slug to navigate to on click. On a normal node this adds a
'view full system' button below its card; on a 'link' node it's the entire point of the card.
label      - linkTo button text override (defaults to name).
terminus   - mainstem only: marks this node as the system's final endpoint (only the 'terminus' node below should set
this).
description - terminus node only: subtitle text.
There's no separate edges list - the main channel line, every tributary/diversion's connector, and flow direction (in
for tributary, out for diversion) are all derived automatically from kind + connectsTo + the order nodes appear above.
The 'bsf_ext' node is where Blacksmith Fork River used to be drawn directly on this page - it's now a 'link' card that
navigates to its own page (blacksmith-fork.json), plus one real preview station ('bsf_before_conf', the last BSF gauge
before it joins the Logan River) chained onto it, with returnsTo pointing back at bsf_confluence so its connector rejoins
the main trunk right where the real confluence is. Do not delete bsf_ext without also removing the reciprocal
'to_lower_logan' link node on that page.
Little Bear River works the same way: 'little_bear' is a 'link' card (right before Spring Creek: Mendon Road) that
navigates to its own page (little-bear.json), which has the full 17-station breakdown. Do not delete it without also
removing the reciprocal linkTo on that page's 'cutler_reservoir' terminus node.
title: Lower Logan River: Valley
subtitle: Dewitt Springs down to Cutler Terminus
